exemplar.title: Exploring the Change in Volume of a Cookie

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear narrative with logical progression from rationale to modelling and reflection
Exceptionally well organized structure with numbered headings and figures
Coherent development allowing the reader to follow arguments without major difficulty
weaknesses: Occasional algebraic slips interrupt the logical flow
Lengthy digressions (camera setup details) reduce conciseness
Repetition of explanations and extensive data tables in the main text hinder relevance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates strong organization (band 4) and generally coherent logical development (band 3), but is held back by issues of conciseness (band 2). Balancing these strands yields a solid band 3 overall for Presentation.

strengths: Mostly consistent and appropriate mathematical language and notation throughout
Highly effective use of multiple representations (algebra, graphs, tables, diagrams) that complement each other
Good logical structure and clear communication of modelling choices and calculus procedures
weaknesses: Inconsistent significant figures and occasional misâtyped derivative reduce precision
Dense formulae without intermediate steps can be hard to follow
Some domain definitions and quotientârule presentations contain typographical errors
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strands for language and clarity sit at band 3, while representations reach band 4. Overall communication is strong yet marred by minor accuracy and clarity issues, justifying a band 3.

strengths: Genuine personal rationale linking baking experiences to mathematical inquiry
Clear evidence of original experiment design and data collection
Creative extension comparing baking soda versus baking powder beyond standard scope
weaknesses: Mathematical techniques stay within familiar IA territory without pushing boundaries
Approach, while personal, does not demonstrate exceptional innovation
Initiative is good but not sustained at an outstanding level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands consistently align with band 2 descriptorsâsignificant independent thinking, a clear personal approach, and good creativityâso the overall score is 2.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on assumptions, model limits and dataâcollection constraints
Thoughtful analysis connecting mathematical results to chemical and practical processes
Clear evaluation of outcomes with suggestions for future improvements
weaknesses: Depth of reflection is moderate rather than substantial or critical
Suggestions for error quantification and technological improvements are not fully developed
Critical analysis stops short of deeper interrogation of algebraic errors
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each strand evidences meaningful but not profound reflection, thoughtful yet not deeply critical analysis, and clear but not comprehensive evaluationâconsistent with band 2 overall.

strengths: Relevant mathematics (integration, regression, optimisation) used effectively and appropriately
Techniques are fully commensurate with HL-level requirements and well applied
Derivations (e.g. volume formula, scale factors, regression constants) show thorough understanding
weaknesses: Several algebraic simplification and sign-handling errors affect accuracy
Derivative calculations contain typographical misâtypes and omitted steps
Oversimplification in modelling cookie shape needs further discussion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands land firmly in band 4: the mathematics is relevant, appropriately levelled, and understood with minor errors. The presence of computational slips prevents a higher band, so Use of Mathematics SL is scored at 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanation of modelling choices and calculus steps is generally clear and sequential.  Occasional algebraic slips (sign errors, omitted powers) and dense formulae without intermediate simplification detract slightly from clarity, so the strand is best characterised as having âgood logical structure and clear communicationâ.

Derivations are mostly correct and show good conceptual understanding.  Some computational slips (incorrect simplification, mis-copied derivative) indicate that complete accuracy is not achieved, so the strand fits âgood understanding and mostly correctâ rather than the error-free descriptors above.

The student employs algebraic derivations, graphs, tables of raw and scaled data, and a clear diagram of the partial sphere.  Each representation complements the others and is well-integrated into the argument (e.g. parameters read from graphs feed into algebra).  This satisfies the âhighly effective and appropriateâ descriptor.

The report follows a clear narrative: rationale â mathematical derivation â modelling â optimisation â reflection.  Arguments are generally logical and each step is built on the previous one, so the reader can follow the development without difficulty.  However, occasional algebra slips and lengthy digressions (e.g. camera-set-up details) slightly interrupt the flow, preventing it from reaching the âexcellentâ descriptor.

Choosing a novel real-life scenario and extending the study to compare baking soda vs baking powder shows creativity and initiative beyond a textbook exercise.  However, modelling techniques and analytical methods follow standard practice, so the investigation fits the âgood level of creativity and initiativeâ band.

The student justifies elimination of extraneous critical points, connects mathematical outcomes to chemical processes, and compares RÂ² values to defend model choices.  Analysis is thoughtful yet not deeply critical; for instance, algebraic errors are not interrogated.  Thus, band 2 is appropriate.

Outcomes are evaluated (e.g. implications for changing baking time, limitations due to oven hot-spots).  Future improvements (checking earlier, cost-saving choice of leavening agent) are mentioned, showing a clear but not comprehensive evaluation, fitting band 2.

Reflection appears throughout: assumptions are questioned, data-collection limitations and model validity after 800 s are acknowledged.  This is âmeaningful reflectionâ, but depth remains moderate rather than âsubstantial and criticalâ, putting it in band 2.

Generally correct terminology (e.g. âvolume of revolutionâ, âquotient ruleâ) and symbolic notation are used.  Minor issues such as inconsistent significant figures and the mis-typed derivative of h(t) show that accuracy is not perfect, yet the language remains appropriate and mostly consistent throughout, fitting band 3.

The context (faulty oven, favourite recipe) is genuinely personal, and the studentâs own data collection strategy is evident.  The approach is clearly theirs but not exceptionally innovative, aligning with âclear evidence of personal approachâ.

Although most material relates to finding the optimum baking time, the write-up is 20 pages (well above IB guidance) and includes repeated explanations, extensive data tables inside the body, and tangential narrative.  These unnecessary elements reduce conciseness, so the work is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ rather than fully concise.

Integration to derive the spherical-cap volume, cubic and rational regression, calculus optimisation and scale-factor analysis demonstrate relevant and effective mathematics, clearly exceeding minimal HL requirements but not reaching the sophistication or rigour expected for âexpertlyâ comprehensive work.

Designing a personal experiment, deciding to model the cookie as a spherical cap, and selecting functions through regression show noticeable independent thinking.  Nonetheless, the mathematics stays within familiar IA territory and does not push boundaries sufficiently for the highest band.

Headings (Part 1, Part 2, Part 3, Reflection, Extensions, Appendices) clearly separate introduction, body and conclusion.  Figures, tables and graphs are sequentially numbered and referenced.  The structure mirrors the objective stated at the outset, making navigation effortless; thus the work meets the âexceptionally well organisedâ standard.

The mathematics (volume of revolution, rational regression, multivariable optimisation) is fully at HL level and appropriately selected for the task.  It is applied with confidence, though not to an exceptional breadth or depth that would justify band 5 or 6.

annotations.comment: The interpretation of Graph 1 identifies concavity changes and links them to ingredient behavior. This effective use of multiple representations enhances understanding.
Solving the cubic system manually increases risk of algebraic errors. Consider using technology to solve the simultaneous equations more efficiently.
The derivative calculation for h(t) appears mis-typed (cubic in derivative). Recompute to ensure correctness of the modelâs critical points.
The reflection on the cubic modelâs negative height domain is perceptive and evaluates the modelâs real-world applicability effectively.
The discussion of cubic model limitations is valuable. Extend this by quantifying potential error bounds to strengthen the evaluation.
The linearization of the width model is executed clearly and supported by an RÂ² comparison, demonstrating effective use of multiple representations.
The derivation of a = e^(â1.96) is correct and concise, but explicitly note the link to ln(a) from the regression intercept for mathematical clarity.
The choice of a rational function with a slant asymptote is well justified by data behavior; this demonstrates appropriate level-commensurate mathematics selection.
The derivative setup for V(t) is dense; consider adding intermediate steps or annotations to enhance logical clarity in the presentation.
The quotient rule simplification for wâ²(t) contains typographical misalignments. Recompute and clearly present numerator and denominator expressions.
The studentâs title is clear and concise, effectively introducing the investigationâs focus on cookie volume changes and setting a coherent tone for the exploration.
The rationale vividly connects personal baking experiences with mathematical inquiry, demonstrating genuine engagement and clear motivation for addressing the oven temperature problem.
The objective outlines the aim but could be refined by specifying the regression methods and modelling strategy for height and width functions to improve clarity.
Assuming the cookie is a perfect partial sphere oversimplifies uneven expansion in the oven; discuss how deviations from spherical shape may impact volume calculations.
The domain for w is mis-specified as ââwâ¤wâ; this should read â0â¤wâ. Correct domain definitions are essential to avoid misinterpretation of variables.
Several algebraic steps in the volume derivation contain sign-handling inconsistencies. Revisit the simplification from the integral evaluation to ensure full mathematical accuracy.
The use of the Pythagorean theorem to express r in terms of w and h is well executed and neatly integrated into the derivation.
The final expression $$V = \tfrac{1}{6}\pi h(3w^2+h^2)$$ is elegantly presented. Highlight its significance before proceeding to data modelling.
The detailed camera-setup description is insightful but interrupts the mathematical flow. Consider placing this in an appendix to maintain conciseness.
The scaling method is clever, but discuss potential sources of error in film-to-real measurements to deepen critical reflection.
Eliminating extraneous roots is logically sound and strengthens the argument that t=675 s is the valid maximum for V(t).
The graphical confirmation of V(t) peaking at (675,144) effectively corroborates the analytical result, demonstrating consistency across representations.
Reflection identifies model limitations well; extend by suggesting how improved camera calibration or repeated trials could reduce scaling errors.
The extension comparing baking soda vs. baking powder shows strong personal engagement and extends the investigation creatively beyond standard IA scope.
The scale factor computation omits the explicit formula and exponent notation (e.g. scale factorÂ³). Include the full equation for transparency and reproducibility.
